items.title: NPR: $50B rural health fund rolls out amid projected Medicaid cuts
items.summary: NPR reports states are planning how to use a $50 billion Rural Health Transformation fund created in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act to offset rural impacts, while the law is projected to cut Medicaid spending by nearly $1 trillion over the next decade. The story highlights how some states may steer hospitals toward service reductions or conversions (e.g., Rural Emergency Hospital status) as they pursue funding.

items.title: Urban Institute projects 4.9M–10.1M fewer Medicaid expansion enrollees in 2028 under new work rules
items.summary: The Urban Institute estimates the One Big Beautiful Bill Act’s Medicaid expansion work requirements and increased redeterminations (starting January 2027) could reduce average monthly Medicaid expansion enrollment in 2028 by 4.9 million (high-mitigation scenario) to 10.1 million (low-mitigation scenario). The analysis also projects 3.0 million–7.0 million coverage losses from work requirements alone, plus 2.0 million–3.1 million fewer enrollees due to six-month redeterminations.

items.title: USDA posts SNAP food restriction waivers with state implementation dates
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP Food Restriction Waivers page lists approved state waivers restricting purchases such as soda/candy and includes target implementation dates. Examples include Florida (target 04/20/26) restricting soda, energy drinks, candy, and prepared desserts; Arkansas (07/01/26) restricting soda and candy; and Texas (04/01/26) restricting sweetened drinks and candy.

items.title: Reuters: SNAP recipients sue USDA over sugary drinks/candy purchase restrictions
items.summary: Reuters reports five SNAP recipients sued USDA to overturn the administration’s efforts to limit SNAP purchases of items like sugary drinks, energy drinks, and candy, challenging USDA’s approval of food restriction waivers for 22 states. Plaintiffs say the restrictions disrupt food access and can create checkout confusion and added out-of-pocket costs for households managing health needs.

items.title: CMS issues bulletin on Medicaid/CHIP managed care monitoring (prior auth, networks, reporting)
items.summary: CMS’ March 12, 2026 informational bulletin highlights state responsibilities to monitor managed care plans, including network adequacy (with a focus on maternal health) and oversight of prior authorization decisions to prevent inappropriate denials and support EPSDT compliance. The bulletin also summarizes required reporting (e.g., MCPAR, MLR Summary, network adequacy reports) and notes upcoming operational milestones like mandatory use of the MC-Review portal for certain submissions after July 1, 2026.

items.title: HUD rescinds/revises multiple COVID-era PIH notices affecting Housing Choice Vouchers and other programs
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-06 (issued March 16, 2026) rescinds numerous COVID-19/CARES Act related PIH notices and revises others, including removing certain LEP-related guidance from PIH 2020-32 and rescinding portions of PIH 2021-14 related to HCV and Public Housing. The notice applies to PHAs operating Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8), Public Housing, and Section 8 Moderate Rehabilitation programs, among others.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: ACF seeks comments on LIHEAP quarterly reports (OMB 0970-0589) changes; deadline April 3, 2026
items.summary: ACF published a Federal Register notice requesting a 3-year extension of the LIHEAP Quarterly Reports (OMB Control No. 0970-0589) and proposing changes intended to reduce redundancy and administrative burden for grantees. Comments are due April 3, 2026, and the notice proposes incorporating data elements from the LIHEAP Carryover and Reallotment Report into the quarterly reporting process.

items.title: USDA requests comments on SNAP National Accuracy Clearinghouse data matching collection; deadline May 4, 2026
items.summary: USDA FNS published a notice inviting public comment on a revised information collection tied to the National Accuracy Clearinghouse (NAC), an interstate data matching system to prevent duplicate SNAP participation across states. Written comments must be received by May 4, 2026, and can be submitted by mail, fax, email, or through Regulations.gov.

items.title: USDA memo: WIC temporarily increases fluid milk maximum monthly allowances through Sept. 30, 2026
items.summary: USDA WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-1 says the FY2026 maximum monthly allowance (MMA) of fluid milk is updated effective immediately, with authority terminating Sept. 30, 2026. The memo lists temporary milk amounts including 16 quarts (Food Packages IV A/B and VI), 22 quarts (Food Package V A/B), and 24 quarts (Food Package VII), and instructs state agencies to maintain updated milk MMAs through Sept. 30, 2026.

items.title: Federal Student Aid posts 2026–27 Pell Grant maximum/minimum award amounts page
items.summary: Federal Student Aid posted a Dear Colleague Letter page for 2026–27 Pell Grant maximum and minimum award amounts (award year July 1, 2026–June 30, 2027). The page indicates the maximum Pell Grant award remains fixed at $7,395 under continuing appropriations, and references a minimum scheduled award of $740 (10% of the maximum, rounded to the nearest $5).

items.title: IRS: 2026 filing season opened Jan. 26; EITC/ACTC refunds expected by March 2 (most direct deposits)
items.summary: The IRS opened the 2026 filing season on Jan. 26, 2026 and said most refunds claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) and Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) should be available by March 2, 2026 for taxpayers using direct deposit with no other issues. The IRS also said Where’s My Refund? should provide projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21, 2026.

items.title: SSA: 2026 SSI federal benefit rate is $994 (individual) / $1,491 (couple); SSDI work thresholds updated
items.summary: SSA’s Red Book ‘What’s New in 2026’ lists the SSI federal benefit rate (FBR) at $994 per month for an eligible individual and $1,491 per month for an eligible couple. It also lists the 2026 trial work period (TWP) monthly earnings amount as $1,210 and substantial gainful activity (SGA) as $1,690 per month for non-blind individuals or $2,830 for blind individuals.
